message: I found out where Anna is. It was a bit too easy, unfortunately.
I don't have any complaints, though.
how should I put this, I kind of wished it was a bit harder.
You wanting to struggle is your business, but I’d rather avoid it. I haven’t lived a life so easy that I long for struggle.
This isn’t the time to be pretentious. Struggle?
It’s fine, you know. Struggle is just right for the detective business.
But, I'm just happy that we got a request!
I ended up buying a weird magazine.
Are you carrying that magazine around?
Welcome back. How did it go with Anna?
The door swung open, and in came Kristy.
Kristy looked around the room and her eyes landed on the magazine Yuriko had been reading.
She thought, but it was already too late.
Your assistant is reading some outrageous magazine, huh?
That's just a gossip magazine. Don't think anything of it.
!? You’ve read it too, haven’t you?
Actually, I have. But it's weird how they can write such nonsense, isn't it?
Perfect. If you understand the situation, come with me.
Where do you mean to date?
Even though the interior has been touched up, its age can't be hidden.
Eventually, it might need a bit more work.
Or rather, it might be better to just move out.
For now, it's my beloved office.
Yuriko is fervently tapping away at her computer keys.
What in the world is she doing? It’s always a mystery.
Maybe she’s even creating a crime database.
Speaking of which, the phone bill being surprisingly high might be because she's using it for communication.
When someone stands in my way, my view gets blocked. I wish they would consider others' inconvenience, but if I said that, I wonder what I might be told.
In this situation, it feels like I’m the one being scolded.
It puts me in a bad mood.
I can’t help but feel pitiful thinking of excuses, but that’s also her
This suit is supposed to be a substitute for the company uniform, so I hear he has a few of the same.
It’s true that uniforms are easy and nice, but for those looking at them, they’re bland and unpleasant.
In big letters on the cover,
it says 'The private life of popular Major League player Guy!'
Hey, is the Boss seriously reading something like this?
I just bought it because I saw Guy's article.
Well then, let’s read right away!
Gai is in a hot love affair with Cindy, and Gai's fiancée was unilaterally told their engagement was over.
Wait, what!? Is this really true?
No way! This has to be a rumor.
Exactly, I mean, it's from a total trash gossip magazine.
It has to be a lie.
Let’s just throw that magazine away. I’ll take care of it.
Are you secretly thinking of reading it?
There's no way that could happen.
It’s fine. I’ll gift you that third-rate magazine.
Huh!? Is that really okay? Boss!
Boss, buying books instead of working?
You’ve got to do well, since it’s a good job.
You have no idea! If Meryl finishes her work smoothly, more work will come pouring in!
That won't happen. You're being dramatic, Yuriko.
Ah, I really need to nail this job.
Maybe if you go back now, she'll be there?
No way, it would be pointless to go right after coming home.
It’s not like I'm just messing around.
Until we meet Anna, we can't expect any progress.
Did you get to see Anna?
You should hurry and visit Anna's place.
Boss, you know where to go, so why not just head out?
Please hurry and get to work.
Let's confirm the matter of the request once more.
The only woman who seems to fit the description is a midwife named Anna, who is of Italian descent.
It's Anna Skakki, who lives in Little Italy.
Little Italy is just around the corner.
That's right. Boss, meet with Anna Skakki, confirm the details about Meryl, and gather some information. Got it?
Honestly, I want to go, but I'm reluctantly giving way to the boss, so make sure you do a good job.
Anyway, the top priority is to meet with Anna.
Boss, please take this matter seriously.
Are you planning to neglect the request?
Meryl's situation doesn’t matter right now. The important thing is to handle Meryl's request.
There's no time to get distracted by women.
What are you planning to do about reuniting with your daughter now? I don't think that person's daughter is the type to warm up right away.
You know, there's apparently a gossip article about Guy in this book, so I decided to buy it.
What kind of article was it?
Well, I haven't read it yet.
Then that's not good, is it?
Anyway, you can't say anything without reading the article.
Right now, the murder case has nothing to do with this.
If you want to spend time on that sort of thing, you should finish the request quickly.
Right now, you can just focus on the request.
The murder case is not related right now.
Actually, this magazine has articles related to that.
Oh, that's a magazine strong on gossip, so of course, they'll pay attention to those topics.
I wonder if they're writing articles that provoke the culprit?
I don't think it's that extreme, right? If you're really curious about the article, you could just read it quickly.
Come on, I really want you to handle the requests properly.
What's going on with the requests?
For now, it's essential to meet with a woman named Anna Skakki first.
I can't move forward without talking to her about Meryl's daughter.
I wonder if she even remembers the whereabouts of a baby from 16 years ago?
It's not hard to get there since it's nearby, but I hope I can meet her next time I go.
I want to see her soon, but things aren't going as I'd like.
